feed.title: Google Cloud: Memorystore for Valkey
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Memorystore for Valkey

entry.content: Memorystore for Valkey has new node types that you can select for your instances. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.summary: Memorystore for Valkey now offers new node types, allowing you to choose the best fit for your instances. This feature is now Generally Available.
entry.title: Feature: New node types for Memorystore for Valkey

entry.content: Version 1.0 of Bloom filters and JSON documents is Generally Available.
entry.summary: Version 1.0 of Bloom filters and JSON documents in Memorystore for Redis is now Generally Available. This feature enhances data handling capabilities within Memorystore.
entry.title: Feature: Bloom filters and JSON documents now GA

entry.content: You can use the Memorystore for Valkey remote MCP server. This server lets you connect to Memorystore for Valkey instances from LLMs, AI applications, and AI-enabled development platforms. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now connect your AI applications and LLMs to Memorystore for Valkey instances using the new remote MCP server. This feature, currently in Preview, allows seamless integration between your AI development platforms and your in-memory data store.
entry.title: Feature: Connect AI apps to Memorystore for Valkey

entry.content:
In addition to the per-instance CA mode, Memorystore for Valkey offers the following new CA modes:

  • Shared CA: a managed, regionalized CA infrastructure. For each region, you can download a single CA certificate bundle. This bundle is valid for all instances located in a region that you configure to use the shared CA. Using a shared CA reduces the number of certificates that clients need to manage. This CA mode is available in Preview.
  • Customer-managed CA: use your own CA pool that's hosted on Certificate Authority Service. If your client applications are configured to trust this CA, then your applications can connect to an instance without you having to download and install additional CA certificates. This gives you greater control and helps you meet compliance requirements. This CA mode is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Memorystore for Valkey now supports Shared CA and Customer-managed CA modes. These new options simplify certificate management and offer greater control over your data encryption in transit. Both modes are currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: New CA modes for Memorystore for Valkey

entry.content: Memorystore for Valkey supports version 1.0 of Bloom filters and JSON documents. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Memorystore for Valkey now supports version 1.0 of Bloom filters and JSON documents. This new feature is currently in Preview, offering enhanced capabilities for data management within Memorystore.
entry.title: Feature: Memorystore for Valkey adds Bloom filters and JSON documents

entry.content: You can use the Google Cloud console to find and set maintenance windows and perform self-service maintenance on instances. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.summary: You can now manage your Valkey instances through the Google Cloud console. This includes setting maintenance windows and performing self-service maintenance, giving you more control over your instance availability. This feature is now Generally Available.
entry.title: Feature: Manage your Valkey instances

entry.content: The simulate maintenance event feature for Memorystore for Valkey is Generally Available.
entry.summary: The simulate maintenance event feature for Memorystore for Valkey is now Generally Available. This feature allows you to test your application's resilience to planned maintenance events.
entry.title: Feature: Simulate maintenance now available

entry.content: You can now deploy instances in the asia-southeast3 (Bangkok), europe-north2 (Stockholm), and northamerica-south1 (Mexico) regions.
entry.summary: You can now deploy instances in three new regions: Bangkok, Stockholm, and Mexico. This expansion offers more options for deploying your applications closer to your users.
entry.title: Feature: New regions available

entry.content: Support for version 9.0 of Valkey is Generally Available.
entry.summary: We are excited to announce that Valkey version 9.0 is now Generally Available. This new version brings enhanced performance and features to your caching solutions. Upgrade today to take advantage of the latest improvements.
entry.title: Feature: Valkey 9.0 support now available

entry.content: You can now use the Google Cloud console to manage backups. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.summary: You can now use the console to manage backups for your Memorystore for Redis instances. This feature is now Generally Available.
entry.title: Feature: Manage Backups Now Available

entry.content: Added support for Valkey version 9.0. As a result, you can now upgrade the version of your Memorystore for Valkey instance to 9.0. For more information, see About upgrading the Valkey version of an instance. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.summary: Memorystore for Valkey now supports version 9.0, allowing you to upgrade your instances. This new feature is currently in Preview. For details on upgrading, refer to the documentation.
entry.title: Feature: Valkey 9.0 Support Added

entry.content: Memorystore for Valkey supports organization policy constraints for customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK). By using these constraints, you can enforce CMEK protection for your instances and limit which Cloud Key Management Service (KMS) keys you can use for this protection. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.summary: Memorystore for Valkey now supports organization policy constraints for customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK). This allows you to enforce CMEK protection for your instances and control which KMS keys can be used. This feature is now Generally Available.
entry.title: Feature: Valkey Instances Now Support Organization Policies for CMEK

entry.content: You can now deploy instances in the africa-south1 (Johannesburg) region.
entry.summary: You can now deploy instances in the africa-south1 (Johannesburg) region. This expansion brings new capabilities closer to users in Southern Africa.
entry.title: Feature: New region available in Africa

entry.summary: The /node/server/healthy metric for Valkey is now Generally Available. This metric provides insights into the health of individual nodes within your Valkey instances.
entry.title: Feature: Valkey Node Health Metric Now Available

entry.summary: A security fix has been implemented for CVE-2025-49844.
entry.title: Feature: Security Fix Deployed

entry.content: You can now use self-service maintenance to update your instance to a newer version. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.summary: You can now use self-service maintenance to update your Memorystore instances to newer versions. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.title: Feature: Self-service maintenance for Memorystore instances

entry.content: You can now create an instance in Memorystore for Valkey, even if a zone of the region where you want the instance to be created is unavailable. If this occurs, then Memorystore for Valkey creates the instance in the available zones of the region. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.summary: Memorystore for Valkey now supports creating instances in available zones within a region, even if the initially selected zone is unavailable. This ensures instance availability and is now Generally Available.
entry.title: Feature: Memorystore for Valkey instances can now be created in available zones when a preferred zone is unavailable.

entry.content:
Memorystore for Valkey now supports maintenance changelogs. Maintenance changelogs provide information about updates available in new maintenance versions, such as patches for security vulnerabilities.

For links to current maintenance changelogs for each major version of Memorystore for Valkey, see Memorystore for Valkey maintenance changelogs. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.summary: You can now view maintenance changelogs for Memorystore for Valkey. This helps you stay informed about updates and security patches.
entry.title: Feature: Memorystore for Valkey maintenance changelogs

entry.content: For each primary node of a Memorystore for Valkey instance, you can now have up to five replica nodes. For more information, see Memorystore for Valkey overview. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.summary: You can now have up to five replica nodes for each primary node of a Memorystore for Valkey instance. This feature is Generally Available.
entry.title: Feature: Valkey instances now support more replicas
